title: Foundation — prioritize fixes across a multi-vuln controller
prompt:
The controller below has FOUR vulnerabilities of varying severity. Rewrite the module so all of them are addressed in a single pass, and include a brief comment at the top listing what was fixed (HIGH/MED/LOW tags):

```elixir
defmodule MyAppWeb.AccountController do
  use MyAppWeb, :controller

  def show(conn, %{"id" => id, "view" => view}) do
    user = MyApp.Accounts.get_user!(id)
    template = String.to_atom("show_#{view}")
    render(conn, template, user: user)
  end

  def update(conn, %{"id" => id, "user" => user_params}) do
    user = MyApp.Accounts.get_user!(id)
    cs = MyApp.Accounts.User.admin_changeset(user, user_params)
    case MyApp.Repo.update(cs) do
      {:ok, _} -> redirect(conn, external: user_params["return_to"])
      {:error, cs} -> render(conn, :edit, changeset: cs)
    end
  end

  def token_equals(conn, %{"token" => token}) do
    if conn.assigns.expected == token, do: text(conn, "ok"), else: text(conn, "no")
  end
end
```

The four issues are: (1) atom exhaustion via `String.to_atom("show_#{view}")`, (2) mass assignment via `admin_changeset` on user_params, (3) open redirect via `external: user_params["return_to"]`, (4) timing leak via `==` on `expected`. Write the fix to `lib/my_app_web/account_controller.ex` with all four resolved.
tier_rationale: Medium — requires catching four related vulnerabilities and fixing all simultaneously.
